- AC2: end-to-end clean loop (reviewer mock APPROVE) under --json emits a final loop_summary line with
  verdict approve, survivingCount 0, exitReason clean, acceptedCount 0, providers listing the reviewer.
  Drive bin/cli.js via the runLoopCli harness pattern (test/loop-providers.test.mjs).
- AC3: end-to-end non-clean exits - no-diff (FLAG + no-op fixer) and no-progress (FLAG + file-changing
  fixer) each emit a loop_summary with verdict needs-attention and survivingCount >= 1, exitReason
  matching. Proves the gatings.length wiring at a >0 site.
- AC4: loop_summary is json-gated - a non-json clean loop prints NO loop_summary to stdout.
- AC5: existing loop_end events are unchanged (their fields/shape); npm test green (no regression).
- AC6 (mutation-proof): reverting verdict derivation to a constant, or dropping acceptedCount:0, or
  removing a call site, fails at least one AC test.

Out of scope: a separate --summary flag (loop_summary rides the existing --json stream); a human
non-json summary line; changing any existing event shape or the single/multi exit codes; automatically
computing acceptedCount (human-owned P6 value).
tickets.title: Emit a consolidated loop_summary NDJSON event at every --loop terminal exit (gate-manifest bridge)

tickets.body:
GitHub issue #10 (ADR-0008 'the one code change worth scheduling'). Today the tool reviews
ONLY a git diff/branch: bin/cli.js restricts scope to auto|working-tree|branch and always calls
collectReviewContext (git). ADR-0008 maps adversarial review onto P1 (design/spec review) and P3
(rail-set adequacy) — neither has a diff to review; they review a document or a declared rail set.
Without a first-class artifact input mode, P1/P3 review degrades to a one-shot manual prompt outside
the tool (no schema, no --verify, no --providers, no loop).

CHANGE: add --input <file(s)> as a review-target mode ALONGSIDE --scope/--base. It reviews arbitrary
artifacts (specs, ticket bodies, rail-set declarations) with the SAME schema, grounding, --verify,
--providers/--quorum, --passes, --findings-ledger, --json, and --prompt-only machinery the diff modes
use — only the review TARGET and the CHARTER differ. The value: P1/P3 review becomes a first-class,
recorded, schema-validated gate instead of an informal prompt.

Concrete changes:
1. src/utils.js parseArgs: add --input (repeatable AND comma-separated; accumulate into args.input as
   a string[]; reject empty entries). Reject --input combined with --base, --scope working-tree|branch,
   and --loop (artifact mode has no git diff / working tree to fix). Document in HELP_TEXT.
2. NEW src/artifact-context.js: collectArtifactContext(cwd, files, {maxBytes}) reads each named file
   (path resolved vs cwd; per-file + total byte budget; binary/oversize/missing guarded), and returns a
   context object SHAPE-COMPATIBLE with git-context: {mode:'artifact', label, content, changedFiles:files,
   includeDiff:true, isEmpty, fileCount, diffBytes, collectionGuidance}. A missing/unreadable file FAILS
   CLOSED (throws) — a gate must never approve because it silently read nothing.
3. NEW prompt-template-artifact.md: an artifact-appropriate adversarial charter (distinct from the diff
   template's 'map the change surface'/'each changed file' framing). Attacks a DESIGN artifact: ambiguity,
   missing/untestable acceptance criteria, unhandled failure modes, unaddressed trust-boundary/security
   concerns, contradictions, unstated assumptions, missing rollback/migration/observability. Same 4
   placeholders ({{TARGET_LABEL}},{{USER_FOCUS}},{{REVIEW_COLLECTION_GUIDANCE}},{{REVIEW_INPUT}}), same
   structured_output_contract + grounding_rules (quote the artifact; cite artifact file+line; file-level
   findings line 0). The output SCHEMA (schema.json) is REUSED UNCHANGED.
4. src/review.js: add buildArtifactPrompt(context, focus) that loads prompt-template-artifact.md via the
   same placeholder substitution as buildPrompt. runReview/runMultiProviderReview/assessFindings/
   mergeProviderResults/renderReport are prompt-agnostic and reused as-is.
5. bin/cli.js: when args.input is set, skip git collection — build the artifact context and the artifact
   prompt, then run the EXISTING single/multi-provider review + grounding + deriveVerdict + render + exit
   path unchanged. --prompt-only prints the artifact prompt. Secret scan still runs on the payload.
6. Skill bundle: NOT bundled (build-time revision). The skill does not wire --input yet, and
   test/skill-assets.test.js is a frozen T2 rail — bundling would be a speculative asset needing a
   rail bypass for no present benefit. prompt-template-artifact.md stays root-only; package.json and
   the skill drift guard are untouched.

Acceptance criteria (concrete verification):
- AC1 (parse + mutual exclusion): parseArgs('--input a.md --input b.md,c.md') => args.input=['a.md','b.md',
  'c.md']; empty value errors; --input with --base OR --scope branch OR --scope working-tree OR --loop
  pushes an error. node --test test/utils.test.js.
- AC2 (collectArtifactContext): reads named files into content with each file's text inlined and
  changedFiles===files, includeDiff===true, isEmpty true only when every input is empty/absent-content;
  a nonexistent file throws. node --test test/artifact-context.test.js.
- AC3 (artifact charter): buildArtifactPrompt substitutes all 4 placeholders (no residual {{...}}),
  embeds the file content in REVIEW_INPUT, and the text is artifact-framed (asserts artifact-review
  language present) and is NOT the diff template (asserts a diff-only phrase like 'changed file' absent).
  node --test test/artifact-context.test.js.
- AC4 (end-to-end): bin/cli.js --input <file> --provider <mock-cli> --json reviews the artifact and emits
  schema-valid JSON; a mock reviewer flagging the artifact => exit 2; approving => exit 0. Drive via a
  mock-CLI harness (test/loop-providers.test.mjs pattern). node --test test/input-mode.test.mjs.
- AC5 (composes): --input --prompt-only prints the artifact prompt (contains the artifact framing + the
  input file content) and exits 0 with no LLM call. node --test test/input-mode.test.mjs.
- AC6 (suite + rail untouched): npm test exits 0; prompt-template-artifact.md is root-only (not bundled)
  and the frozen rail test/skill-assets.test.js is unchanged.
- AC7 (mutation-proven): reverting bin/cli.js to buildPrompt (diff template) for --input fails AC3/AC5;
  dropping any --input mutual-exclusion check fails AC1; making a missing input file read as empty instead
  of throwing fails AC2. One-time manual, recorded.

Out of scope: --loop with --input (no working tree to fix — v1 rejects it); changing schema.json;
changing the diff-based modes or their exit codes; auto-detecting artifact vs diff (explicit --input only);
reviewing directories/globs (explicit file paths only in v1).

--- P5 ADVERSARIAL-REVIEW HARDENING (2 distinct families, corroborated) ---
The live P5 review (claude+codex) found collectArtifactContext could exit 0 'approve' having reviewed
NOTHING when every --input file was empty/binary/oversized/over-budget (isEmpty -> cli.js empty-scope
exit 0) — a fail-OPEN. Fixed: collectArtifactContext now FAILS CLOSED (throws, exit 1) when no reviewable
content remains; there is no isEmpty=true path for artifacts. Also switched lstatSync->statSync so the
size cap follows symlinks (target, not the tiny link), and budgets on actual bytes read. New tests:
all-empty/binary(NUL@0)/oversize-symlink -> throw; E2E empty artifact -> exit 1. AC2 revised accordingly.
tickets.title: Add --input artifact review mode (review specs/tickets/rail-sets, not just git diffs)

tickets.body:
GitHub issue #12. Today --loop rejects --scope branch and --base (src/loop.js runLoop scope block):
the fixer writes to the working tree and the loop stashes/pops to checkpoint, which only works for
uncommitted working-tree review. So --loop structurally cannot drive a PRE-MERGE branch-vs-base
convergence loop (review a feature branch, apply fixes as commits, re-review until clean) — arguably
the most common real use case (ADR-0007's 18-round Cursor case study was driven MANUALLY for lack of
this).

DESIGN DECISION (owner-approved): the loop touches ONLY the current feature branch; the base (main) is
READ-ONLY (diff reference). Rollback uses git reset --hard on the FEATURE BRANCH — approved because it
never rewrites main. Confirmed acceptable even if the feature branch is pushed (warn, do not refuse).

APPROACH: add a SEPARATE runBranchLoop(cwd, args) rather than refactoring the working-tree loop, so the
shipped/tested working-tree path is byte-for-byte unchanged (zero regression risk). runLoop dispatches to
it when args.scope==='branch' || args.base. It REUSES the leaf helpers (runProviderRound / single-provider
review, getGatingFindings, gatingSetsEqual, buildFixPrompt, getFixFiles, redactSecretsInFindings,
spawnFixer, buildFixerCmd, detectFixer, probeOsConstraint, buildLoopSummary, emitEvent).

BRANCH-LOOP MECHANICS:
1. Precondition: CLEAN working tree at start (git status --porcelain empty) — else error. This is what
   makes commit/reset/clean safe: any file appearing mid-loop is fixer-created.
2. Resolve base like collectReviewContext (args.base or upstream/origin-HEAD/main). Record originalHead =
   git rev-parse HEAD (the whole-loop checkpoint / recovery target). Refuse if HEAD is detached.
3. Each round: review <branch> vs <base> via collectReviewContext({scope:'branch', base}); compute gatings
   exactly like the WT loop (single or --providers quorum).
4. Clean (no gatings) -> exit 0, fix commits stay. no-progress (gating set repeats) / ceiling (loop-max)
   -> exit 2, LEAVE the fix commits (partial progress), print 'git reset --hard <originalHead>' recovery.
5. Before each fix: beforeFixHead = git rev-parse HEAD. Spawn the fixer (writes working tree).
   - fixer error/timeout: git reset --hard beforeFixHead && git clean -fd (discard the failed partial),
     exit 2. Prior rounds' commits remain; print reset-to-originalHead recovery.
   - fixer made NO change (working tree still clean): no-diff, exit 2, print recovery.
   - fixer made changes: git add -A && git commit -m 'fix: resolve adversarial-review findings (round N)'
     -> the commit is the branch progress the next round re-reviews.
6. SIGINT: kill fixer, print originalHead + 'git reset --hard <originalHead>' recovery, exit 1 (NEVER
   auto-reset on interrupt — let the human decide).
7. Pushed-branch warning: if the branch has an upstream, warn that reset will diverge local from remote
   (needs a force-push to reconcile) — but proceed.
8. Emit the same NDJSON events (loop_start/review/review_result/fix/loop_end) + the consolidated
   loop_summary, with a git-checkpoint field (originalHead / commit shas) instead of stashRef.

Acceptance criteria:
- AC1 (dispatch + clean-tree precondition): runLoop with --scope branch (or --base) routes to runBranchLoop;
  a DIRTY working tree at start errors (exit 1) without committing/resetting anything. node --test test/branch-loop.test.mjs.
- AC2 (converge = append commits, main untouched): a branch with a fixable finding + a fixer that resolves
  it -> loop commits the fix on the BRANCH and re-review approves (exit 0); base branch ref is unchanged
  (same sha before/after); HEAD advanced by the fix commit(s). node --test test/branch-loop.test.mjs.
- AC3 (fixer error rolls back this round only, never main): fixer writes partial then exits non-zero ->
  git reset --hard beforeFixHead + clean, exit 2; base ref unchanged; no partial commit remains. node --test.
- AC4 (no-progress/ceiling leave commits + recovery): reviewer keeps flagging + fixer commits each round ->
  exit 2 with exitReason no-progress or ceiling, the fix commits REMAIN on the branch, and a
  'git reset --hard <originalHead>' recovery line is printed. node --test.
- AC5 (main is never a write target): across every exit path, assert the base branch's sha is identical
  before and after the loop (the loop only ever moves HEAD of the feature branch). node --test.
- AC6 (loop_summary parity): branch-loop emits a terminal loop_summary with providers/iterations/verdict/
  exitReason/survivingCount/acceptedCount under --json. node --test.
- AC7 (WT loop unchanged): the working-tree loop path and its tests are untouched; npm test green. node --test.
- AC8 (mutation-proven): dropping the clean-tree precondition fails AC1; resetting to the WRONG ref (e.g.
  base instead of beforeFixHead/originalHead) fails AC3/AC5; committing on the base ref fails AC5.

Out of scope: squashing/amending the fix commits (left as individual commits; user squash-merges);
operating on a scratch worktree (reset-on-branch approved); auto force-push; --input + branch-loop;
rewriting or writing to main under ANY path.
tickets.title: Support --loop for branch scope (pre-merge convergence loops, commit-mode fixer + reset checkpoints)

tickets.body:
GitHub issue #22. Three test files drive the real bin/cli.js end-to-end in throwaway git repos with mock CLIs on PATH: test/loop-providers.test.mjs, test/loop-summary.test.mjs, test/branch-loop.test.mjs. Each test spawnSyncs `node bin/cli.js ...` inside a fresh mkdtemp git repo (init, commits, and — for branch mode — add/commit/reset --hard/clean). npm test is `node --test`, which runs test files in a worker pool (parallel across files).

Symptom: under parallel CPU load, running the loop test files together intermittently fails on git/subprocess contention. Observed during the #12 (T7) prosecution: combined runs reported fail=2 on ~2 of ~20 runs, only while system load was elevated by a concurrent gate run. The flake once broke the adlc hollow-test gate's own baseline ("baseline suite is not green"), so it can intermittently block the P5 mutation gate.

Not a regression: reproduces on main (T6 prosecutor independently observed it under induced 3x load, before #12 existed). #12 widens the contention surface but does not cause it.

Root cause: many rapid git subprocesses across concurrent throwaway repos under load produce transient failures (e.g. index.lock contention, fs races). The harness treats every git call as infallible, so a single transient non-zero exit surfaces as a hard test failure.

DECISION: harden the harness git calls (fix option 2 from the issue) rather than serializing test files — a shared retry helper directly addresses the root cause (transient git contention) with zero wall-clock cost to the suite, versus test-concurrency=1 which was measured to add material wall-clock and only reduces contention probability rather than eliminating the fragility. Add a small shared spawn-retry helper used by all three harnesses' inline `git()` closures: bounded retries (with backoff) on transient git failures during setup/teardown git calls only. Do NOT retry the assertions being tested (the CLI subprocess's own exit code/output) — only the git plumbing calls the harnesses use to build/mutate the throwaway repos.

Acceptance criteria:
- AC1 (deterministic under load): the three loop test files pass N consecutive runs (e.g. 20) while a CPU-stressor runs in parallel (e.g. `yes >/dev/null &` xcpu), with 0 failures. Verify: a loop invoking `node --test test/branch-loop.test.mjs test/loop-summary.test.mjs test/loop-providers.test.mjs` under induced load, 20x.
- AC2 (gate baseline stable): `adlc hollow-test --test-cmd "npm test" --base main` obtains a green baseline on the first attempt across 5 runs under load (no "baseline suite is not green" aborts).
- AC3 (no coverage lost): the full suite still runs all files and stays green (npm test = current count, all pass); no test is skipped or weakened to hide the flake; the retry helper only wraps git plumbing calls, never the assertions under test.
- AC4 (wall-clock acceptable): npm test wall-clock is materially unchanged from baseline (no test-concurrency reduction), since the fix is retry-on-transient-failure, not serialization.

Out of scope: changing what the loop tests assert; rewriting the harnesses to avoid spawning the real binary; serializing the test suite.
tickets.title: De-flake the spawn-heavy loop e2e tests under parallel load (blocks P5 gate)

tickets.body:
Replace the broken `--provider cursor` path (hardcoded OpenAI-compatible
proxy at http://127.0.0.1:8765/v1 — a third-party `cursor-api-proxy` convention,
NOT a Cursor product surface; no proxy is started by Cursor IDE) with the official
Cursor Agent CLI (`agent` on PATH, also shipped as `cursor-agent`).

INVESTIGATION FINDINGS (P0, 2026-07-16):
- Current code in src/llm.js sets apiBase=http://127.0.0.1:8765/v1, apiKey="dummy",
  model=gpt-4o when provider=cursor. Local probe: connection refused. Auto-detect
  inside TERM_PROGRAM=cursor falls back to this dead proxy when no API keys/CLIs
  are present — reviews fail closed with a misleading Cursor-proxy story.
- Official Cursor Agent CLI (`agent`, v2026.05.05+) supports non-interactive review:
  `agent -p --mode plan --output-format text|json --model <id> [--trust] [prompt]`.
  `--mode plan` (or `ask`) is read-only; bare `-p` WITHOUT --mode "Has access to all
  tools, including write and shell" — MUST force plan/ask for adversarial review
  isolation (mirrors claude/agy `--permission-mode plan` and codex `--sandbox read-only`).
  Auth: `agent login` or CURSOR_API_KEY. Models via `--model` / `agent models`.
- `@cursor/sdk` (Agent.prompt) can do one-shot reviews but is OUT OF SCOPE: this
  package is zero-dependency (package.json has no dependencies); do not add @cursor/sdk.
- Third-party localhost proxies remain OUT OF SCOPE as a first-class provider.
  Users who run one can already use `--provider openai --api-base http://127.0.0.1:8765/v1`.

RESOLVED DESIGN DECISIONS:
- D1 Transport = local CLI, not HTTP API. `--provider cursor` (and alias token
  `agent`) resolves to provider="cli", cliCmd="agent" (prefer `agent` over
  `cursor-agent` if both exist). Remove the knownApis "cursor" HTTP path and the
  127.0.0.1:8765 defaults entirely.
- D2 Invocation argv (review isolation):
    agent -p --mode plan --trust --output-format text [--model <m>] -
  Prompt via stdin (`-` sentinel if supported; else argv fallback with size guard,
  same pattern as callCliLLM). If `--allow-unsandboxed-cli`, omit `--mode plan`
  (document that this re-enables write/shell tools — dangerous for untrusted diffs).
  Prefer `--mode ask` only if plan is rejected by an older agent; do not silently
  drop to unsandboxed `-p`.
- D3 Family / multi-provider: keep `cursor` OUT of TOKEN_FAMILY / FAMILY_CANDIDATES
  for `--providers` diversity (subscription can route to openai/anthropic models —
  counting it as an independent family would fake diversity). Single-provider and
  explicit `--provider cursor` / `--provider agent` remain supported. Auto-detect
  inside TERM_PROGRAM=cursor: prefer independent critics first (unchanged order),
  then fall back to `agent` CLI (with same-provider warning) instead of the dead proxy.
- D4 Default model: omit `--model` unless args.model is set (let Cursor account
  default apply). If args.model is set, pass `--model <args.model>`.
- D5 JSON extraction: agent has no --output-schema; reuse cleanJsonResponse on the
  printed text (same as claude/agy). --output-format json wraps metadata — prefer
  text output and scrape JSON from the assistant message body (document + test).

CONCRETE CHANGES:
1. src/llm.js configureLLM: remove cursor from knownApis HTTP branch; map
   provider tokens `cursor` and `agent` to cliCmd resolution (isCmdInstalled("agent")
   then "cursor-agent"). Update Cursor-env auto-detect fallback from provider=cursor
   HTTP to cliCmd=agent. Update error messages to mention `agent`.
2. src/llm.js cliReviewArgs / cliFallbackArgs / callCliLLM: special-case
   cliCmd in {agent, cursor-agent} with the D2 argv; do NOT reuse Claude's
   `--permission-mode plan` (Cursor uses `--mode plan`).
3. src/loop.js FIXER_PROVIDER_MAP: map agent/cursor-agent -> "cursor" (or omit
   from fixer auto-detect if fixer write tools are required — prefer NOT auto-
   selecting agent as --loop fixer because plan mode can't write; document:
   agent is reviewer-only unless --allow-unsandboxed-cli + explicit --loop-fixer).
4. src/utils.js HELP_TEXT: document `--provider cursor|agent` as local Cursor
   Agent CLI; remove implication of a built-in local HTTP proxy.
5. README.md + both SKILL.md copies: replace "Cursor's local proxy" docs with
   Agent CLI prerequisites (`agent` on PATH, login or CURSOR_API_KEY).
6. CHANGELOG.md: note the breaking change (HTTP 8765 path removed; use openai
   --api-base for third-party proxies).
7. Tests: update configureLLM cursor tests; add agent argv unit tests (mock
   binary asserts --mode plan -p present; asserts no call to 8765).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (concrete verification):
- AC1: `configureLLM({provider:"cursor"})` with `agent` on mocked PATH returns
  {provider:"cli", cliCmd:"agent"} and does NOT set apiBase to 127.0.0.1:8765.
  Verify: node --test test/llm.test.js.
- AC2: TERM_PROGRAM=cursor auto-detect with no API keys and only `agent` on PATH
  selects cliCmd=agent (with same-provider warn). Verify: test/llm.test.js.
- AC3: llmCall for cliCmd=agent invokes argv containing `-p`, `--mode`, `plan`
  (or `--plan`), and does not invoke unsandboxed tool mode by default. Mock binary
  records argv. Verify: node --test test/llm.test.js.
- AC4: `--allow-unsandboxed-cli` omits `--mode plan` / `--plan` for agent (parity
  with claude/agy unsandboxed escape hatch). Verify: unit test.
- AC5: `grep -n "8765" src/` returns no hits after the change (proxy default gone).
  Verify: shell grep in CI/test or a dedicated assertion test.
- AC6: `--providers` family tables still exclude cursor/agent (no fake diversity).
  Verify: existing multiprovider tests stay green; add assert that resolve of
  token `cursor` is NOT used by `--providers auto`.
- AC7: npm test exits 0.

OUT OF SCOPE: adding @cursor/sdk; bundling/starting any localhost proxy; making
cursor a `--providers` family; changing schema.json; Vercel AI Gateway (T10).
tickets.title: Replace broken Cursor local-proxy provider with official `agent` CLI

tickets.body:
Emerged from dogfooding during T11: every review of this repository pulled .adlc/tickets.json (68 KB) and docs/ prose into the payload alongside a ~200-line source change, inflating the diff to ~181 KB across 10+ files.

THREE OBSERVED HARMS, all seen for real during T11:
1. Timeouts. The first multi-provider run failed outright — both providers exceeded the 120s default --timeout on the oversized payload. The review could not complete at all.
2. Diluted attention. Reviewer effort spent on generated ticket JSON and planning prose is effort not spent on the source change.
3. False findings. Round 5 produced a CRITICAL/security finding against .adlc/tickets.json, correctly observing that its text ('deliberately excluded from T11 scope') is repository data attempting to steer review scope. By the letter of the trust rules that is right — which is precisely why our own generated planning artifacts should not be in the payload.

There is currently NO way to exclude paths. --scope and --base select a range, not a file set.

IMPLEMENTATION:
1. src/utils.js: add --exclude <glob>, repeatable and comma-separated, parsed into an array; document in HELP_TEXT.
2. src/git-context.js: filter excluded paths out of the changed-file list and the inlined diff, for working-tree, branch and --input modes alike.
3. Excluded files MUST still be visible, never silently dropped: report them in the existing coverage.files_skipped channel and in the human report, so a reviewer can tell the difference between 'reviewed and clean' and 'not reviewed'.
4. Consider a documented default exclusion set for known-generated paths (lockfiles, .adlc/). If a default is added it must be overridable and must be announced in output — a silent default exclusion is a gate weakness, since an attacker who knows the default can hide changes in an excluded path.

SECURITY NOTE — the sharp edge of this feature: an exclusion mechanism is a way to hide code from the reviewer, which is the same primitive as the span-deletion bug T11 fixed. Exclusions must therefore be operator-controlled, never derived from repository content (a .adversarialreviewignore file committed to the repo under review would let an attacker exclude their own malicious diff). Keep exclusions on the command line or in operator config outside the reviewed tree.

OUT OF SCOPE: a config-file mechanism (none exists; see T13 notes on --profile); changing --max-files/--max-bytes defaults; the fence or trust policy.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- AC1: --exclude accepts repeated and comma-separated globs. VERIFY: unit test in test/utils.test.js asserts parseArgs collects both forms into one array, and HELP_TEXT documents the flag.
- AC2: Excluded paths are absent from the changed-file list and the inlined diff. VERIFY: test in test/git-context.test.js builds a context over a repo with matching and non-matching files and asserts only the non-matching ones appear.
- AC3: Exclusions are reported, not silent. VERIFY: test asserts excluded paths appear in coverage.files_skipped and in the rendered report.
- AC4: Exclusions cannot be sourced from the reviewed repository. VERIFY: test asserts that a committed ignore-style file in the repo under review has no effect on what is reviewed.
- AC5: Any default exclusion set is announced in output and overridable. VERIFY: test asserts the default set is listed in output and that passing an explicit flag replaces it.
- AC6: Full suite green. VERIFY: npm test exits 0.
tickets.title: Add --exclude <glob> so generated artifacts do not dominate the review payload
